concerns.text: The 'Data Compliance & Ethics Statement' (Appendix) claims adherence to Common Crawl Terms and Robots Exclusion Protocol but lacks specific details on the opt-out mechanism for copyright holders. The text states 'If any owner... believes... inappropriate, please contact us' but does not provide a dedicated email address, a takedown URL, or a specific process for handling DMCA-style requests. This is a critical gap for a dataset distributed publicly.

The paper mentions human expert evaluation (Appendix: Details of Expert Evaluation) and states experts were compensated. However, it fails to explicitly state whether the study received IRB (Institutional Review Board) approval or an exemption determination. Given the involvement of human subjects (even for expert annotation), this is a standard requirement for publication in reputable venues and must be explicitly addressed.

The dataset includes documents from 'Gov & Legal' and 'Medical & Health' domains (Table 1, Appendix). While sourced from Common Crawl, the paper does not detail the specific safeguards taken to ensure no Personally Identifiable Information (PII) or Protected Health Information (PHI) was inadvertently included in the 711 selected PDFs. A statement on PII scrubbing or exclusion criteria for sensitive documents is required.
